perturbation.original_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. Both parties acknowledge and agree that the disclosing party and/or its third party vendors (as the case may be) own all rights, title and interest in the Confidential Data. Both parties further acknowledge and agree that the unauthorized disclosure of the Confidential Data will cause irreparable harm to the disclosing party. As a result of the unique nature of the Confidential Data, in addition to all other remedies available, the disclosing party shall be entitled to seek injunctive and other extraordinary relief in a court of competent jurisdiction in order to enforce the receiving party's obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. While parties acknowledge the disclosing party may own rights to Confidential Data, any request for injunctive relief is subject to mandatory arbitration before seeking court intervention. Injunctive relief will only be considered if irreparable harm is demonstrated and monetary damages are provably insufficient. The parties agree that the burden of proof for both is on the disclosing party.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause grants the disclosing party the right to seek immediate injunctive relief for unauthorized disclosure. The modified clause mandates arbitration before court intervention, effectively delaying and complicating the process. This creates a contradiction because the original clause promises quick action, while the modified version imposes a condition precedent. If the data disclosure is time sensitive, it contradicts the assumption that a harmed party can immediately seek relief.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6. INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. While parties acknowledge the disclosing party may own rights to Confidential Data, any request for injunctive relief is subject to mandatory arbitration before seeking court intervention. Injunctive relief will only be considered if irreparable harm is demonstrated and monetary damages are provably insufficient. The parties agree that the burden of proof for both is on the disclosing party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in section 6 introduces a mandatory arbitration clause before injunctive relief can be sought, which directly contradicts the original text's implication of immediate access to injunctive relief. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, emphasizes the requirement for arbitration, irreparable harm demonstration, and insufficient monetary damages before considering injunctive relief. This creates a clear contradiction with the original implication of immediate availability, fulfilling the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' by making the availability of injunctive relief unclear and subject to preconditions not initially stated.

perturbation.original_text: 7. OTHER PROVISIONS. The parties further agree that: (a) this Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Nebraska; (b) this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and none of the terms of this Agreement may be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties; (c) a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by written waiver duly signed by such party, and no failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of such right; (d) this Agreement shall inure only to the benefit of the parties hereto, and the rights and obligations of each party under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the consent of the other party; (e) no provision of this Agreement shall affect, limit or restrict either party's right to engage in any business in any place and at any time, whatsoever, provided the receiving party does not disclose the Confidential Data in violation of this Agreement; (f) each party agrees not to advertise, or otherwise make known to others, any <PAGE> 78 information regarding this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship except as may be required by law; (g) neither party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Data disclosed hereunder; (h) the invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement; (i) all notices under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered to and received by a party, and will otherwise become effective, on the date of actual delivery thereof (by personal delivery, express delivery service or certified mail) to the Notice Address of such party set forth below; (j) this Agreement may be executed in counterparts; and (k) this Agreement is dated for all reference purposes October 14, 1998.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. OTHER PROVISIONS. The parties further agree that: (a) This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware; (b) this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and none of the terms of this Agreement may be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties; (c) a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by email communication duly acknowledged by the other party, and no failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of such right; (d) This Agreement shall not inure to the benefit of any third party, and the rights and obligations of each party under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the consent of the other party; (e) No provision of this Agreement shall affect, limit or restrict either party's right to engage in any business in any place and at any time, whatsoever, provided the receiving party does not disclose the Confidential Data in violation of this Agreement; (f) Each party agrees not to advertise, or otherwise make known to others, any <PAGE> 78 information regarding this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship except as may be required by law; (g) Neither party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Data disclosed hereunder; (h) The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement; (i) All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered to and received by a party, and will otherwise become effective, on the date of actual delivery thereof (by personal delivery, express delivery service or certified mail) to the Notice Address of such party set forth below; (j) this Agreement may be executed in counterparts; and (k) This Agreement is dated for all reference purposes October 14, 1998.
perturbation.explanation: The agreement originally stated that the law is Nebraska law. Changing it to Delaware creates a fundamental contradiction. Moreover, the contract originally states 'a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by written waiver duly signed by such party'. Altering this to a party may wave rights by email and duly acknowledged by the other party is another contradiction. This creates legal uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. OTHER PROVISIONS. The parties further agree that: (a) This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware; (b) this Agreement sets forth the entire agreement and understanding between the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof, and none of the terms of this Agreement may be amended or modified except by a written instrument signed by both parties; (c) a party may waive any rights under this Agreement only by email communication duly acknowledged by the other party, and no failure to exercise or delay in exercising a right under this Agreement shall constitute a waiver of such right; (d) This Agreement shall not inure to the benefit of any third party, and the rights and obligations of each party under this Agreement may not be assigned or delegated without the consent of the other party; (e) No provision of this Agreement shall affect, limit or restrict either party's right to engage in any business in any place and at any time, whatsoever, provided the receiving party does not disclose the Confidential Data in violation of this Agreement; (f) Each party agrees not to advertise, or otherwise make known to others, any <PAGE> 78 information regarding this Agreement or the Proposed Relationship except as may be required by law; (g) Neither party makes any representations or warranties as to the accuracy or completeness of any Data disclosed hereunder; (h) The invalidity or unenforceability of any provision of this Agreement shall not affect the validity or enforceability of any other provision of this Agreement; (i) All notices under this Agreement must be in writing and shall be deemed to have been delivered to and received by a party, and will otherwise become effective, on the date of actual delivery thereof (by personal delivery, express delivery service or certified mail) to the Notice Address of such party set forth below; (j) this Agreement may be executed in counterparts; and (k) This Agreement is dated for all reference purposes October 14, 1998.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 7(a) to state that Delaware law governs the agreement, while the original text specified Nebraska law. Since the contradicted text and changed text are exactly the same, this means it still explicitly states that Delaware Law governs the agreement. Thus, the change indeed creates a contradiction with the original terms of the contract where Nebraska law was to govern the agreement. Additionally, section 7(c) is modified to allow waivers by email, while the original stated waivers must be in writing and signed. Since the changed text and contradicted text are the same, this creates an internal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. PUBLIC DATA. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, Data shall not be, or shall cease to be, Confidential Data hereunder: (a) if such Data is known to the receiving party prior to disclosure thereof by the disclosing party; (b) after such Data is published or becomes available to others, without restriction and without breach of this Agreement by the receiving party; (c) after such Data becomes available to the receiving party from others having no obligation to hold such Data in confidence; or (d) if such Data is developed by the receiving party independently of any disclosure of such Data by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. PUBLIC DATA. Notwithstanding any other provision of this Agreement, Data shall not be, or shall cease to be, Confidential Data hereunder ONLY if such Data is subject to a formal patent application that has been published by a government authority. Data known to the receiving party, publicly available data, or data independently developed is still considered confidential under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of Public Data excludes several cases such as already know to the receiver or publicly available. With this change, it drastically narrows what constitutes Public Data to *only* data subject to a formal, published patent application. This contradicts the spirit of intellectual property and open-source usage. For example, software using Apache 2.0 now becomes Confidential and owned by the providing party and not free to use, according to the new definition.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. NO ADDITIONAL RIGHTS. The receiving party shall have the right to use Confidential Data in ways not expressly prohibited by this Agreement, as long as such use does not directly compete with the disclosing party's core business. Without limiting the generality of any other provision of this Agreement: (a) no license is hereby or otherwise granted, directly or indirectly, under any patent, copyright or other proprietary right of the disclosing party or its third party vendors; and (b) neither party shall be obligated to disclose Data to the other party or to enter into any further agreements relating to the Proposed Relationship or Data. A party may terminate discussions regarding the Proposed Relationship at any time. The receiving party shall, upon written request of the disclosing party, return to the disclosing party all Confidential Data, including all copies thereof, disclosed hereunder. The receiving party's obligations under this Agreement respecting the Confidential Data shall survive termination of said discussions.
perturbation.justification: The changed text drastically narrows the definition of 'Public Data' compared to the original text, effectively making previously considered public or independently developed data confidential. This directly contradicts Section 5, which grants the receiving party the right to use Confidential Data in ways not expressly prohibited, as now using something like Apache 2.0 would violate that section, given it would now be considered Confidential data, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1. DISCLOSURE. The parties intend to engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship (the "Proposed Relationship"). In connection therewith, Level 3 and NNT may disclose to each other technical, financial and/or other information, material, or data which is written, oral or in any other form, electronic or otherwise (collectively "Data") which is considered confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. DISCLOSURE. The parties intend to engage in discussions concerning a potential business relationship (the "Proposed Relationship"). In connection therewith, Level 3 and NNT may disclose to each other technical, financial and/or other information, material, or data which is written, oral or in any other form, electronic or otherwise (collectively "Data"). It is understood that only technical Data is to be considered confidential and proprietary.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes technical, financial, and/or other information as "Data" which is confidential and proprietary. Modifying this to specify that ONLY technical data is confidential creates a contradiction. The implications of the change now mean financial and other types of information are not restricted. But in Section 2, the definition of Confidential Data included financial information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. CONFIDENTIAL DATA. "Confidential Data" means (a) Data explicitly marked as 'Confidential' at the time of disclosure by the disclosing party, and (b) any analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on explicitly marked 'Confidential' Data disclosed by the disclosing party. Data is only considered Confidential if it's physically stamped or digitally watermarked as such.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Data' as including technical, financial, and other information which is considered confidential. The changed text narrows this definition to state that only technical data is confidential. This directly contradicts Section 2, which defines 'Confidential Data' as including data explicitly marked confidential, encompassing analyses, compilations, studies, summaries, extracts or other documentation prepared by the receiving party based on explicitly marked 'Confidential' Data, which might include financial information. Since financial information is no longer considered confidential under the modified definition in Section 1, this creates a contradiction because Section 2 still treats it as potentially confidential if marked as such.
